source.document: CXS 154-1985 - Standard for Whole Maize (Corn) Meal

reference.document: CXS 154-1985 - Standard for Whole Maize (Corn) Meal

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text:
1.1 This standard applies to whole maize (corn) meal for direct human consumption prepared from kernels of common maize, Zea mays L., as described in Section 2.1.
1.2 This standard does not apply to degermed maize (corn) meal, enriched maize (corn) meal, maize (corn) flours, maize (corn) grits, quick grits, hominy grits, self -rising maize (corn) meals, bolted maize (corn) meals, maize (corn) flakes and other maize (corn) based ready -to-eat cereals, maize (corn) flaking grits, and alkaline treated maize (corn) products.
1.3 This standard does not apply to maize meals for use as a brewing adjunct, to maize meals used for manufacturing of starch and any industrial use, nor to maize meal for use as an animal feed.

page_spans.text:
1.1 This standard applies to whole maize (corn) meal for direct human consumption prepared from kernels of common maize, Zea mays L., as described in Section 2.1.
1.2 This standard does not apply to degermed maize (corn) meal, enriched maize (corn) meal, maize (corn) flours, maize (corn) grits, quick grits, hominy grits, self -rising maize (corn) meals, bolted maize (corn) meals, maize (corn) flakes and other maize (corn) based ready -to-eat cereals, maize (corn) flaking grits, and alkaline treated maize (corn) products.
1.3 This standard does not apply to maize meals for use as a brewing adjunct, to maize meals used for manufacturing of starch and any industrial use, nor to maize meal for use as an animal feed.

source.document: CXS 154-1985 - Standard for Whole Maize (Corn) Meal

reference.document: CXS 154-1985 - Standard for Whole Maize (Corn) Meal

sections.title: DESCRIPTION
sections.text: Whole maize (corn) meal is the food prepared from fully mature, sound, ungerminated, whole kernels of maize, Zea mays L., by a grinding process in which the entire grain is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness. In its preparation, coarse particles of the ground maize kernel may be separated, reground and recombined with all of the material from which they were separated.

In its preparation, coarse particles of the ground maize kernel may be separated, reground and recombined with all of the material from which they were separated.

page_spans.text: Whole maize (corn) meal is the food prepared from fully mature, sound, ungerminated, whole kernels of maize, Zea mays L., by a grinding process in which the entire grain is comminuted to a suitable degree of fineness. In its preparation, coarse particles of the ground maize kernel may be separated, reground and recombined with all of the material from which they were separated.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969)1 and other codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product.
5.2 To the extent possible in good manufacturing practice, the product shall be free from objectionable matter.
5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
– shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health;
– shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and
– shall not contain any substance originating from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health.

reference.document: CXS 154-1985 - Standard for Whole Maize (Corn) Meal

sections.title: PACKAGING
sections.text:
6.1 Whole maize (corn) meal shall be packaged in containers which will safeguard the hygienic, nutritional, technological, and organoleptic qualities of the product.

6.2 The containers, including packaging material, shall be made of substances which are safe and suitable for their intended use. They should not impart any toxic substance or undesirable odour or flavour to the product.
6.3 When the product is packaged in sacks, these must be clean, sturdy and strongly sewn or sealed.

sections.title: Quality factors – general
sections.text:
3.1.1 Whole maize meal shall be safe and suitable for human consumption.
3.1.2 Whole maize meal shall be free from abnormal flavours, odours, and living insects.
3.1.3 Whole maize meal shall be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non-retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

classification.sentence: In its preparation, coarse particles of the ground maize kernel may be separated, reground and recombined with all of the material from which they were separated.
classification.pattern: main clause with modal verb 'may' and coordinated verbs
classification.justification: Sentence grants permission for processing steps with 'may'; moderate template similarity due to modal permission and coordination.
notes: Permission expressing multiple allowed actions.

sentences.original: 5.1 It is recommended that the product covered by the provisions of this standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene  (CXC 1-1969)1 and other codes of practice recommended by the Codex Alimentarius Commission which are relevant to this product.

classification.sentence:
5.3 When tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination, the product:
– shall be free from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health;
– shall be free from parasites which may represent a hazard to health; and
– shall not contain any substance originating from microorganisms in amounts which may represent a hazard to health.
classification.function: Quality and safety requirements expressed as obligations
classification.pattern: conditional clause (when tested ...) + bullet list of shall/shall not obligations

components.condition: when tested by appropriate methods of sampling and examination
requirements: free from microorganisms which may represent health hazard
free from parasites which may represent health hazard
not contain substances originating from microorganisms which may represent health hazard

classification.justification: Complex conditional obligations with multiple clauses coordinated; structure partially matches typical safety requirement templates.
notes: Obligations under testing conditions, complex multi-item list.

sentences.original: ASH MAX: 3.0% on a dry weight basis Refer to Section 8 PROTEIN (N x 6.25) MIN: 8% on a dry weight basis Refer to Section 8 CRUDE FAT MIN: 3.1% on a dry weight basis

classification.sentence: Refer to Section 8 GRANULARITY 95% or more of the whole maize meal shall pass through a 1.70 mm sieve – and – 45% or more shall pass through a 0.71 mm sieve – and – 35% or less of the whole maize meal shall pass through a 0.212 mm sieve Refer to Section 8
classification.pattern: Reference + Parameter + Numeric Threshold + Subject + shall + pass through + Specification + Conjunction + ...

thresholds: 95% or more of the whole maize meal shall pass through a 1.70 mm sieve
45% or more shall pass through a 0.71 mm sieve
35% or less of the whole maize meal shall pass through a 0.212 mm sieve

classification.justification: The sentence imposes multiple granularity obligations with quantitative thresholds using 'shall pass through' structure, partially matching compositional limits templates.
notes: Complex numeric conditions linked by conjunctions

pages.title: STANDARD FOR WHOLE MAIZE (CORN) MEAL
pages.summary: Página inicial do documento com título principal em caixa alta e cabeçalho CXS 154-1985. Contém apenas texto introdutório, sem subdivisões.
Página apresenta um título em caixa alta sem numeração como nível 1 e um parágrafo relacionado a ele. Não há outras subdivisões nesta página.

Página apresenta uma hierarquia clara com seções numeradas de 1 a 6 e subseções numeradas correspondentes. Textos são associados corretamente a seus respectivos títulos e subtítulos. A seção 6 continua na próxima página.

A página contém continuação da seção 6 (seções 6.2 e 6.3), nova seção 7 com subseções 7.1 e 7.2, e a seção 8. Textos estão corretamente associados aos títulos. A seção 6 vem da página anterior e termina nesta página. Seções 7 e 8 começam e terminam nesta página.

Página contém três seções principais: ANNEX, Factor/Description Limit Method of analysis e NOTES. Cada seção possui texto explicativo associado. Não há títulos numerados nesta página. Todas as seções começam e terminam nesta página. O cabeçalho inclui código e número da página.
